name: Server-Side Request Forgery (Cloud Metadata Access)
description: Tests for SSRF by injecting cloud metadata endpoint URLs into parameters that accept URLs. Detects when internal cloud metadata services (AWS, GCP, Azure) are accessible through the application.
author: AC3 ScanForge
remediation: Validate and sanitize all user-supplied URLs. Block requests to internal IP ranges (169.254.x.x, 10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, 192.168.x.x). Use allowlists for permitted external domains. Disable unnecessary URL fetch functionality.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: SSRF to cloud metadata is the #1 technique for stealing IAM credentials in cloud environments. The Capital One breach (2019) was caused by SSRF accessing AWS metadata to obtain temporary credentials.
